mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling while CPIAUCSL remains elevated, markets infer easier policy and a lower expected path for short rates; the long end follows via reduced expected average policy rates, so the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS declines on the latest monthly observation but the 10-year yield rises over the next 20 trading days in most triggered instances, or if the sign relation is inverted in a rolling 2-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals labor-market cooling and slower nominal growth expectations; that should push the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 20 trading days as term premiums and expected policy rates fall.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp from its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE crosses the threshold but the 10-year yield rises over the next 20 trading days in more than 60% of such episodes, or if PAYEMS continues accelerating and nullifies the labor-softening effect.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both strengthen together, the market reads this as resilient consumption and income growth that lifts real-rate expectations; that joint demand impulse should raise the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous monthly improvement in RSAFS and PAYEMS is followed by lower 10-year yields in a majority of cases, or if either series alone explains the effect and the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO after a prior lull indicates improving supply utilization and stronger cyclical momentum; as output expectations firm, the 10-year yield should rise over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive monthly prints
failure_condition: Reject if two straight monthly gains in INDPRO are not followed by positive 20-day yield changes in at least 55% of cases, or if the response appears only after 60 days rather than within 20 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp downturn in HOUST signals weakening housing demand and lower construction-linked growth expectations; that should reduce expected real rates and push the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if a housing-starts drop larger than 5% fails to precede lower 10-year yields, or if GDP growth remains strong enough that the yield direction is consistently positive despite weak HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: If CPIAUCSL is still accelerating while FEDFUNDS is unchanged, the market should price a higher future policy path; that repricing lifts the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS
regime_hint: inflation surprise without immediate policy response
failure_condition: Reject if inflation acceleration above the policy rate is not followed by higher 10-year yields, or if the yield response disappears when FEDFUNDS is already tightening quickly.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-tightening labor cooldown, a rising UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS creates a clear recession-risk signal; investors should seek duration, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rising unemployment paired with payroll deceleration does not lead to lower yields within 20 trading days, or if the effect only appears in quarter-ahead horizons and not at 20 days.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is weak and RSAFS is also soft, the demand outlook deteriorates enough to compress expected real rates and term premium; the 10-year Treasury yield should fall over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth is below 1% and RSAFS is below its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if weak GDP and retail sales fail to precede lower 10-year yields in most triggered episodes, or if the sign flips positive during recessionary demand shocks.
